description: Applies the §11.1 version acceptance and refusal rules: refuses a higher major openbindings version than the tool supports (and, while the spec is pre-1.0, a higher minor); compares by SemVer 2.0.0 precedence so build metadata is ignored and a higher patch within a supported minor is accepted; and does not accept a prerelease unless the tool declares support for that specific prerelease.
notes: Tests assume the SDK under test supports a version range bounded below `999.x` for major-version refusal cases, and bounded below `0.999.x` for pre-1.0 minor-version refusal cases. SDK conformance to OBI-T-04 is independent of which specific version the SDK supports; the test asserts the refusal behavior on documents declaring versions higher than any plausible SDK release in this version family. Lower-version cases are generally not tested because OBI-T-04 does not mandate a specific behavior for documents declaring a lower minor than the SDK supports. Three SemVer-comparison cases per §11.1 are tested against an SDK supporting the `0.2.x` family: build metadata is ignored (`0.2.0+build.1` is accepted as `0.2.0`); a higher patch within a supported minor is accepted (`0.2.1`); and a prerelease, which sorts below its release, MUST NOT be accepted unless the tool declares support for that specific prerelease (`0.2.0-rc.1` asserts default rejection by a tool that has not opted in; the opt-in exception is not separately fixtured).

tests.description: document declares the current spec version (SDK should accept assuming it supports 0.2.x)
build metadata is ignored for comparison; `0.2.0+build.1` is treated as `0.2.0` and accepted
a higher patch within a supported minor is accepted as non-breaking
a prerelease is not accepted unless the tool declares support for that specific prerelease (default rejection)
document declares a major version higher than any plausible SDK supports
document declares a far-future major version with non-zero minor
pre-1.0 document declares a higher minor than any plausible SDK supports (per §11.1 finer-granularity rule)
pre-1.0 document declares a higher minor with a patch component
post-1.0 forward-compat: document declares a same-major-higher-minor version (SDK supporting major 1 should accept per §11.1)
